 {"id":"bd-1o4","title":"E2E CLI scenarios: tool enable/disable + error paths","description":"Goal:
- Add E2E tests that validate tool enable/disable behavior and common error paths with detailed logging.

Scope:
- `--tools` selection impacts available tool list in system prompt.
- `--no-tools` disables all tools and confirms tool-call failures are surfaced.
- Error paths: invalid model/provider, missing API key, rpc mode restrictions.
- Validate that error messages are clear and actionable.

Logging Requirements:
- Use CLI E2E harness (bd-1wc) + TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log effective tool list, system prompt excerpt, VCR_MODE, and cassette name/path.
- Capture stdout/stderr and assertion diffs on failure.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests capture stdout/stderr and confirm error messages.
- Logs show tool registry inputs + effective tool list + prompt excerpts + cassette id.
- No network access; VCR playback used where streaming needed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:59:09.623038612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:42:58.950250159Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:00:32.353467259Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-1wc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:00:27.455303348Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:59:09.629601854Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-30u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:42:58.950223199Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:01:01.836681493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1pb","title":"Perf: Baseline + optimization plan (alien-grade rigor)","description":"# Goal
Establish a **profile-driven optimization loop** for the PiJS runtime and connectors.

# Method (Extreme Optimization)
1) Baseline: `hyperfine --warmup 3 --runs 10` on key extension workloads.
2) Profile: `cargo flamegraph` for CPU, heaptrack for allocations.
3) Prove: golden outputs + isomorphism checklist for every change.
4) Implement: only opportunities with score ≥ 2.0.
5) Verify: sha256 of golden outputs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Benchmark scripts for representative extensions.
- Opportunity matrix with impact/confidence/effort.
- Initial flamegraph and top hotspots.

# Output
- A short performance report that ties back to `bd-btq` success criteria.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:23:45.780267200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:26.236946942Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pb","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:26.236917347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1pb","depends_on_id":"bd-2ke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:25.317622080Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1pb","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:25.777714572Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1pf","title":"Set up VCR-style test infrastructure for provider streaming","description":"# Set up VCR-style test infrastructure for provider streaming

## Goal
Create infrastructure to record and replay real API responses for provider testing.

## Background
VCR (Video Cassette Recorder) testing pattern:
1. First run: Make real API call, record response to file
2. Subsequent runs: Replay recorded response, no network

This gives us:
- Real response format testing (not simplified mocks)
- Deterministic CI (no flaky network tests)
- Real error response testing (rate limits, auth failures)

## Implementation

### Recording Mode
```rust
// tests/vcr/mod.rs

pub struct VcrRecorder {
    cassette_path: PathBuf,
    mode: VcrMode,
}

pub enum VcrMode {
    Record,    // Make real calls, save to cassette
    Playback,  // Replay from cassette, fail if not found
    Auto,      // Playback if exists, else record
}

impl VcrRecorder {
    pub fn new(test_name: &str) -> Self { ... }

    pub async fn request(&self, req: Request) -> Response {
        match self.mode {
            VcrMode::Record => self.record_and_return(req).await,
            VcrMode::Playback => self.playback(req),
            VcrMode::Auto => ...
        }
    }
}
```

### Cassette Format
```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "test_name": "anthropic_streaming_text",
  "recorded_at": "2026-02-02T...",
  "interactions": [
    {
      "request": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages",
        "headers": { ... },
        "body": { ... }
      },
      "response": {
        "status": 200,
        "headers": { ... },
        "body_chunks": [
          "event: message_start\ndata: {...}\n\n",
          "event: content_block_delta\ndata: {...}\n\n",
          ...
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### SSE Streaming Support
For streaming responses, store individual chunks:
- Each SSE event as separate chunk
- Preserve timing information (optional replay delay)
- Support partial responses (stream interruption)

### Sensitive Data Redaction
```rust
pub fn redact_cassette(cassette: &mut Cassette) {
    // Replace API keys with placeholder
    cassette.redact_header("x-api-key", "[REDACTED]");
    cassette.redact_header("authorization", "[REDACTED]");

    // Replace sensitive response data
    cassette.redact_body_field("api_key", "[REDACTED]");
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) to log record/playback actions.
- Log cassette path, mode, request summary (method/url), and redaction summary.
- On playback mismatch, log expected vs actual interaction keys + diff hints.

## Files to Create
- tests/vcr/mod.rs - VCR infrastructure
- tests/vcr/cassette.rs - Cassette format/loading
- tests/vcr/recorder.rs - Recording logic
- tests/vcr/playback.rs - Playback logic
- tests/fixtures/vcr/ - Directory for cassette files

## Testing the VCR System
- Unit test: Load cassette from JSON
- Unit test: Match request to interaction
- Unit test: Redaction works
- Integration: Record + playback cycle

## Environment Variables
- VCR_MODE=record|playback|auto
- VCR_CASSETTE_DIR=path/to/cassettes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VCR infrastructure compiles
- [ ] Recording mode saves cassettes
- [ ] Playback mode replays cassettes
- [ ] SSE streaming supported
- [ ] Sensitive data redacted
- [ ] Record/playback actions logged with detail
- [ ] CI uses playback mode only

 {"id":"bd-3ml","title":"Implement verbose test logging infrastructure","description":"# Implement verbose test logging infrastructure

## Goal
Create shared infrastructure for detailed logging in all integration tests,
enabling easy debugging when tests fail.

## Background
E2E and integration tests need detailed logging to debug failures:
- Every test action should be logged
- Timestamps for timing analysis
- Context (file paths, values) for reproduction
- Logs dumped automatically on test failure

## Implementation

### Log Infrastructure
```rust
// tests/common/logging.rs

use std::sync::Mutex;
use std::time::Instant;

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct LogEntry {
    pub timestamp: Instant,
    pub level: LogLevel,
    pub category: &'static str,
    pub message: String,
    pub context: Vec<(String, String)>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum LogLevel {
    Debug,
    Info,
    Warn,
    Error,
}

pub struct TestLogger {
    entries: Mutex<Vec<LogEntry>>,
    start: Instant,
}
```

### Redaction + Artifacts
- Add helpers to redact sensitive values in env/header/context keys (API keys, auth headers).
- Add a simple artifact registry: `record_artifact(name, path)` so tests can attach outputs.
- On panic, dump logs + artifact list (paths) to stderr.

### Test Harness Integration
```rust
// tests/common/harness.rs

pub struct TestHarness {
    pub logger: TestLogger,
    pub temp_dir: TempDir,
}

impl Drop for TestHarness {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if std::thread::panicking() {
            eprintln!("\n=== TEST LOGS ===\n{}", self.logger.dump());
            eprintln!("=== ARTIFACTS ===\n{}", self.logger.dump_artifacts());
        }
    }
}
```

### Optional File Output
- If `TEST_LOG_PATH` is set, write logs to that path in addition to stderr.

## Files
- tests/common/mod.rs
- tests/common/logging.rs
- tests/common/harness.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TestLogger captures all levels
- [ ] Context key-values supported
- [ ] Timestamps included
- [ ] Redaction helpers present
- [ ] Artifact registry present
- [ ] Auto-dump on test failure
- [ ] Optional file output works
- [ ] All integration tests use harness","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:54.070756084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:48:33.124493922Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:48:33.124430233Z","close_reason":"Implemented redaction helpers, artifact registry + dump, and TEST_LOG_PATH file output in test logging; harness now dumps artifacts on panic and provides record_artifact. Acceptance criteria satisfied.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ml","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:54.080417065Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":16,"issue_id":"bd-3ml","author":"QuietBear","text":"Reviewed existing tests/common logger/harness: core logging already present but missing redaction, artifact registry, and TEST_LOG_PATH dump. Implementing those now in tests/common/logging.rs + tests/common/harness.rs (record_artifact, dump_artifacts, redaction helper, env-based file output, artifact dump on panic).","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:48:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3nz","title":"Wire session indexing into session.persist()","description":"# Wire session indexing into session.persist()

## Goal
Automatically update the SQLite index when sessions are saved.

## Implementation

### Add Index Update to persist()
```rust
// src/session.rs

impl Session {
    pub async fn persist(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
        // Existing JSONL write logic
        self.write_jsonl(path).await?;
        
        // NEW: Update index
        if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
            if let Err(e) = index.index_session(path) {
                // Log warning but don't fail persist
                tracing::warn!("Failed to update session index: {}", e);
            }
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Default Index Path
```rust
// src/session_index.rs

impl SessionIndex {
    pub fn open_default() -> Result<Self> {
        let path = dirs::data_dir()
            .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("."))
            .join(".pi/agent/sessions/index.db");
        Self::open(&path)
    }
}
```

### Handle Index Creation
- Create index.db if not exists
- Use WAL mode for concurrent access
- Handle permission errors gracefully

## Considerations
1. **Performance**: Index update should be fast (<10ms)
2. **Reliability**: Don't fail persist if index fails
3. **Concurrency**: Handle multiple pi instances
4. **Migration**: Handle existing sessions without index entries

## Testing
- Test: persist() updates index
- Test: persist() succeeds even if index fails
- Test: Index contains session after persist

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] persist() calls index_session()
- [ ] Index created if not exists
- [ ] Errors logged but don't fail persist
- [ ] Performance impact <10ms","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:32.093344392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:26.066864853Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:06.998331380Z","close_reason":"Completed: Session::save() now updates SessionIndex (best-effort); index stored under sessions root; redundant index_session calls removed; test added.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nz","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:32.099125014Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-3nz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Picking up bd-3nz. Plan: make Session::save() update SessionIndex (best-effort: warn-only, never fails save), move index DB under the sessions root for easy isolation, and remove redundant SessionIndex::index_session calls from AgentSession + interactive save paths.  {"id":"bd-h04","title":"Extensions: connector dispatcher + capability policy","description":"Background:
- Connector dispatcher is the security boundary for all extension runtimes (WASM/JS).
- It defines the canonical host_call/host_result protocol and enforces capability policy.

Scope:
- Implement dispatcher core, capability checks, error mapping, and structured audit logging per the logging spec.
- Provide hooks for policy modes (strict/prompt/permissive) and test harness instrumentation.

Steps:
- Define connector message types + validation; map to internal tool/command/env/fs/http calls.
- Implement allow/deny decision flow with reason codes; enforce timeouts + cancellation (asupersync).
- Emit structured logs (correlation IDs, capability, decision, elapsed).
- Provide deterministic test hooks/mocks for unit + conformance tests.

Acceptance:
- All hostcalls route through the dispatcher with consistent policy enforcement.
- Logs follow the extension logging schema and redact secrets.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:23:49.329723505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:14:35.827372808Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:14:35.827308398Z","close_reason":"Dispatcher: policy enforcement, timeout handling, redacted logging, test hooks","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h04","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:57:23.511568828Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"bd-h04","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented hostcall timeouts + redaction + deterministic log overrides in connector dispatcher.  {"id":"bd-hqq","title":"Unit tests: compaction pipeline + summary invariants","description":"# Unit tests: compaction pipeline + summary invariants

## Goal
Add comprehensive tests for compaction logic to ensure summaries are correct,
message windows are respected, and session invariants hold — without mocks.

## Scope
- Compaction window selection (token budget, message count, system prompt rules).
- Summary insertion semantics (where summary is injected, ordering preserved).
- Handling of tool calls, thinking blocks, and image/content blocks.
- Edge cases: tiny budgets, already-compacted sessions, empty assistant response.
- Session integrity: parent/child IDs, current leaf, and compaction entry metadata.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log input message count, token budget, selected span indices, and summary length.
- On failure, dump the compacted timeline (message IDs + types + brief text).

## Determinism Requirements
- Use fixed fixtures for token counts and message content (no randomness).
- No network usage; temp dirs only if persistence is exercised.

## Files
- tests/compaction.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ compaction tests
- [ ] Window selection respects budget and role ordering
- [ ] Summary entry inserted correctly
- [ ] Tool + thinking blocks preserved or summarized as specified
- [ ] Session tree invariants preserved
- [ ] Logs include compaction selection + summary metadata","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:29.310486765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T09:16:21.984938067Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T09:16:21.984876392Z","close_reason":"Implemented compaction tests + invariants; gates green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hqq","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:29.317387163Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hqq","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:34.801055578Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-idw","title":"E2E CLI scenarios: session lifecycle (continue/resume/export)","description":"Goal:

 {"id":"bd-iub","title":"Spec refresh: extensions/resources + parity audit","description":"Background:
- Porting-to-rust is spec-first; EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE.md is the authoritative spec for implementation.
- Legacy behavior lives in legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/* (extensions.md, packages.md, skills.md, prompt-templates.md, rpc.md, settings.md, themes.md, models.md, session.md, tui.md, keybindings.md).
- Extension approach is defined in EXTENSIONS.md (tiers A/B/C, connector model, QuickJS event loop, artifact pipeline, log schema).

Scope / Steps:
1) Re-read legacy docs + examples/extensions to extract exact behavior, defaults, validation, and edge cases.
2) Update EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE.md sections for resources/extensions/UI/RPC with precise message shapes + cancellation semantics.
3) Update FEATURE_PARITY.md: add missing entries and mark gaps discovered; ensure each gap maps to a bead.
4) Update PLAN_TO_COMPLETE_PORT.md with any sequencing changes uncovered.

Acceptance:
- EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE has enough detail to implement extensions/resources without consulting legacy code.
- FEATURE_PARITY reflects all gaps discovered in this audit with references to bead IDs.
- Plan doc updated only where new gaps/ordering were discovered.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:25.586816474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:25.595085284Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iub","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:25.595057311Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-wdo","title":"Crate publish readiness audit (pi + libs)","description":"Background:
- We plan to publish pi_agent_rust, asupersync, rich_rust, charmed_rust to crates.io with a tag-driven workflow.
- Rust-crates-publishing skill requires metadata (license/readme/repo), tag/version alignment, and dry-run validation.

Scope / Steps:
1) Audit each Cargo.toml: package metadata, readme path, repository URL, keywords/categories, publish flag.
2) Ensure versioning and tag strategy (vX.Y.Z matches package.version).
3) Add/update GitHub Actions publish job gated on tags + CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN.
4) Run cargo package/publish --dry-run for each crate (order deps first).

Acceptance:
- All crates pass dry-run publish locally.
- CI publish workflow is in place and gated to release tags.
- Version/tag alignment documented in README or RELEASE.md.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:08.527044029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:08.543413705Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wdo","depends_on_id":"bd-3tu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:08.543375474Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xgo","title":"Extensions: extc pipeline (SWC) + cache + compat scanner","description":"Background:
- JS/TS extensions need a deterministic build pipeline to run as-is without Node/Bun.
- The pipeline must rewrite legacy APIs into connector hostcalls and enforce compatibility.

Steps:
- Implement extc build pipeline with SWC: bundle, tree-shake, and emit target artifact (QuickJS bytecode or JS->WASM).
- Run a static compatibility scan (forbidden APIs, Node/Bun globals, unsafe patterns).
- Apply protocol shims/rewrite passes to map legacy APIs to pi connector calls.
- Add artifact cache keyed by sha256(manifest + bundle + engine_version).
- Emit build report metadata (warnings, compatibility flags, required capabilities).

Logging requirements:
- Build logs must include extension id, input hash, and any rejected APIs.

Acceptance:
- A representative JS extension bundles deterministically with cache hits on repeat.
- Compatibility violations are reported with actionable diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:24:33.029915380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:37.753660024Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xgo","depends_on_id":"bd-2ki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:36.827771763Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xgo","depends_on_id":"bd-3bs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:37.294416003Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xgo","depends_on_id":"bd-3sf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:37.753633053Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xgo","depends_on_id":"bd-576","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:57:27.809161367Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xgo","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:25:02.648692771Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
